title: Euonymus kewensis
intro: Euonymus kewensis, also known as Kew spindle or Spindle tree, is a deciduous shrub native to China, characterized by its distinctive red berries and fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Euonymus kewensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestion and relieve menstrual cramps.
taxonomy: Euonymus kewensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to China and is a type of spindly shrub or small tree. The plant belongs to the genus Euonymus, which includes a variety of species commonly known as spindle trees or burning bushes. Euonymus kewensis is closely related to other species in the genus, such as Euonymus japonica and Euonymus fortunei. These plants are known for their vibrant autumn colors and distinctive seed pods.
